commanded_lines: Action envelope: read exact-head product sources; generate scratch probes only under /tmp/dkr-p2-calibration; write only the authorized checkpoint.v1.json and progress.finish.json records; no product, frame, tree, ledger, release, or external-state changes.
One-shot rule: every allocation, retained-memory, row, and provenance probe ran once; rejected or unaccepted results were preserved without favorable repetition.
Accepted worker evidence: exact-head clean-build provenance, Node 26 retained-object controls 0/0/1, and one scope.run owned lifecycle row.

Rejected worker evidence: the inspector allocation method measured 269 and 501.5 attributed bytes per iteration for controlled 1 KiB and 2 KiB allocations and therefore failed the byte-accounting contract.
Unaccepted rows: the managed ctx.exec probe used current ownership instead of default boundary ownership, and post-session introspection resolved a new current-owned session runtime.
Downstream decision: fund one reducing allocation-method DKR with thresholds declared before execution; do not implement measurement or optimize.
Standing walls: every unread or independently unverified wall blocks downstream advance.
DKRs are discovery-worker scopes; PKRs are progression-worker execution units; there is no CKR worker.
payload.next_check_at: Independent validator dispatch within the frame freshness window.
payload.result: Exact-head clean-build provenance and a pinned Node 26 retained-object method produced valid worker evidence. The allocation method was rejected, two row probes remain unaccepted, and performance implementation stays blocked.

wall_gate.reason: The one-shot inspector sampler did not account for the known 1 KiB controls, the corrected managed-context and session lifecycle rows still require replay, and independent validation is absent.
